intro: I am interested in the Senior Data Engineer contract role supporting Player and Data Analytics. My background fits the core need for Python, SQL, Spark-oriented data pipelines, data modeling, and turning high-volume signals into reliable analytics products.
body: At Citi, I architected Python and Pandas ETL pipelines that ingested P95 telemetry from 6,000+ enterprise endpoints and converted operational signals into trusted reporting and forecasting datasets. I also worked with AWS S3, Glue, Redshift, Oracle, and PySpark to support large-scale analytics, historical retention, and executive reporting. That experience maps well to building robust pipelines for logs, events, and behavioral signals.

The role emphasizes data quality, reliability, usability, and stakeholder confidence. Those are areas where I have strong hands-on experience: validation checks, reconciliation, data cleansing, repeatable reporting pipelines, metric definitions, and collaboration with business and engineering stakeholders. In prior performance and observability roles, I worked closely with teams to align on metrics, thresholds, dashboards, and actionable insights from telemetry data.

While my direct domain is banking, hospitality, telecom, and enterprise infrastructure rather than video games, the underlying data problem is familiar: high-volume event signals, business-facing analytics, reliable pipelines, and clear definitions that help teams make decisions. I can bring senior data engineering judgment, strong Python and SQL skills, and practical analytics delivery to a fast-moving player analytics environment.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my data engineering, telemetry analytics, and business-facing pipeline experience can support this team.
